title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall, marking his second collaboration with the orchestra.
The concert featured American music themes, including Broadway and jazz pieces like 'It Don't Mean a Thing' and 'Yankee Doodle.'
Odom Jr. performed songs from his career, including 'Wait for It' from Hamilton, and covered 'A Change is Gonna Come.'

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, from Chelsea, faces 12 charges for allegedly assaulting two children under 14.
Prosecutors accuse Bautista-Zepeda of using a cigarette lighter and heated fork to burn the children.
Bautista-Zepeda was held without bail after pleading not guilty; a dangerousness hearing is set for May 28.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, from Concord, was convicted of rape and indecent assault at a Sandwich restaurant in 2023.
Jammal received 13.5 to 15 years in state prison for rape, followed by 5 years probation for the assault.
The Barnstable County court cited Jammal's actions as motivated by a 'shocking sense of entitlement' before sentencing.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A historic Newton church built in 1893 will be converted into six condominium units at 19 Highland Ave, Newtonville.
A separate five-story building with 26 additional condos will be added to the site by Habitech Communities.
Habitech acquired the property for $4 million after previous plans to convert it into an arts hub fell through.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: NBA insider Michael Scotto reports Kawhi Leonard could be available for trade; Celtics may consider him for his defensive prowess.
LeBron James, set to be a free agent, remains a potential long shot for the Celtics due to his scoring and playmaking abilities.
Boston could explore adding center Jaxson Hayes to bolster depth, given Neemias Queta's playoff struggles.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series for the first time after beating Molloy University in Waltham.
Endicott College reached the Division III championship round for the fourth consecutive year by defeating Salve Regina University.
Bentley and Endicott will compete in their respective NCAA World Series tournaments in North Carolina and Ohio.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Celtics plan to work out UConn star Alex Karaban before NBA Draft, per David Borges of CT Insider.
Karaban, from Southborough, ranked No. 32 on ESPN's top 100 list, won two NCAA championships with UConn.
Celtics hold the No. 27 and No. 40 picks in the draft with Karaban averaging 12.5 points at UConn.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall in Fenway showcasing a blend of jazz and pop from his 'Whatever's Clever!' album.
Puth incorporated nostalgic hits and highlighted his Berklee College of Music ties, referencing Boston as his 'second home.'
Opening acts Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey set the stage, with Seavey impressively live-looping and performing an Olivia Rodrigo cover.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full at The Cabot in Beverly, celebrating its 30th anniversary.
Jakob Dylan and the band followed each track with songs from Tom Petty's 1982 album 'Long After Dark.'
The set concluded with an encore featuring Wallflowers' tracks and Tom Petty covers like 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting.'

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston saw the largest population decline in Massachusetts, losing 1,338 residents last year, totaling 5,644 fewer since 2020.
Everett and Worcester experienced the highest population gains, with increases of 2,362 and 1,916 residents, respectively.
Massachusetts' population growth slowed to 0.2%, with housing costs and reduced immigration cited as key factors.
